channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: ESL Partners With Lamborghini for DreamHack Events into 2028
item.content:encoded:
Esports tournament organisers ESL FACEIT Group have entered a long-term partnership with Automobili Lamborghini.

The luxury sports car brand has become the Official Automotive Partner for DreamHack festivals in the US and Europe through to 2028.

Lamborghini previously held activations at DreamHack Dallas and DreamHack Stockholm in 2025 and will continue at future events.

The appearances at last year’s festivals included the premiere of the Vesaro x Automobili Lamborghini racing simulators and the Lamborghini Revuelto, the first “Lamborghini High Performance Electrified Vehicle”, with a DreamHack-specific livery.

What Does Lamborghini Have Planned for DreamHack?

Lamborghini will be at this month’s DreamHack Atlanta, running May 15-17, with two Temerario cars wrapped in bespoke gaming-inspired liveries — a Temerario Play Louder Livery and a Temerario Fast Forworld created in Fortnite livery.

The two cars with gaming designs will be driven through Atlanta in the lead-up to the festival before arriving on-site in the Creator Hub for photo opportunities.

Lamborghini will also be part of DreamHack Atlanta’s sim racing challenges and will host additional activations across the event.

“Lamborghini is not just a partner, but a key enabler in elevating the experience for fans, players and creators through innovation and premium integration,” says Daniel Mauritz, SVP Brand Partnerships at ESL FACEIT Group.

“We are proud to become the main automotive partner of this event, marking an important step in bringing Lamborghini closer to new generations,” states Christian Mastro, marketing director at Automobili Lamborghini.

“Through the worlds of gaming and digital, we aim to translate the Lamborghini dream into new, immersive experiences for a highly passionate and dynamic audience… we want to engage fans and allow them to experience our brand in a new and meaningful way,” Mastro added.

What’s your all-time favourite Lamborghini car? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Additionally, DreamHack is confirmed to return to Birmingham in 2027 after the success of its Midlands debut earlier this year.

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: League of Legends Patch 26.10 Buffs and Nerfs
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has released League of Legends (LoL) Patch 26.10 with some interesting champion and item changes.

Lee Sin has received a rather substantial adjustment, Gluttonous Greaves have been nerfed, and Arena now features teams of three.

Plus, Riot keeps refreshing the game with things such as Discord integration and champion keybinds introduced in the last LoL Dev Update.

Here are all the nerfs and buffs from the latest League of Legends patch.

Minion Aggro

In a rather substantial change, Riot has changed one of the fundamental parts of the game.

Now, attacking enemy minions will no longer put your champion into the minions’ aggro priority list.

Lee Sin Adjustment

Riot Games has made some big changes to Lee Sin, whose mobility is now outclassed by more modern champions.

Riot hopes these changes will allow Lee Sin players to make more plays, with the addition of Lee sending dead bodies flying an especially entertaining change.

However, time will tell whether this was a nerf or a buff.

  • W – Safeguard
    • Shield: 70 / 115 / 160 / 205 / 250 (+80% AP) ⇒ 60 / 105 / 150 / 195 / 240 (+ 80% AP)
    • Flat Cooldown: 12s when used on non-champions and 6 when used on champions ⇒ 7s at all points
    • (New) Shield on non-champions: Lee Sin gets his shield when jumping to a minion or ward
  • E – Tempest
    • Magic Damage: 35 / 60 / 85 / 110 / 135 (100% AD) ⇒ 35 / 60 / 85 / 110 / 135 (90% AD)
  • R – Dragon’s Rage
  • (New) Unconditional Collateral: A champion’s body will continue to fly, knock up, and do damage even if killed mid-flight

LoL Patch 26.10 Champion Buffs

Ambessa

Apparently, the nerfs from the last patch have given Riot some room to make Ambessa stronger in the top lane rather than the jungle.

Riot is boosting her early power while leaving her jungle clear and late game potential untouched.

  • Q – Cunning Sweep / Sundering Slam
    • Target Max Health Damage: 2 / 3 / 4 / 5 / 6% ⇒ 4 / 4.5 / 5 / 5.5 / 6%
    • Bonus Monster Damage: 125 ⇒ 75
  • R – Public Execution
    • Healing Percentage: 10 / 12.5 / 15% ⇒ 15 / 17.5 / 20%
    • Healing Effectiveness vs Monsters: 40%⇒ 25%

Galio

Mid lane quest changes and the removal of Phase Rush have hurt Galio quite a bit.

So, Riot is buffing the Demacian in a number of areas, including making his ult now scale with his primary stat: magic resistance.

  • Q – Winds of War
    • Mana Cost: 70 / 75 / 80 / 85 / 90 ⇒ 60 / 65 / 70 / 75 / 80
  • E – Justice Punch
    • Total Damage: 90 / 130 / 170 / 210 / 250 (+90% AP) ⇒ 100 / 135 / 170 / 205 / 240 (+100% AP)
  • R – Hero’s Entrance
  • (New) Total Damage: Now scales with 100% bonus magic resistance

Quinn

Quinn has been struggling top, so Riot is buffing the mana costs of her ult and opening the door to her becoming a jungler.

  • P – Harrier
    •  (New) Bonus Monster Damage: 50
  • Q – Blinding Assault
    • (New) Bonus Monster Damage: Now deals 50% increased damage to monsters
  • R – Behind Enemy Lines
    • Mana Cost: 100 / 50 / 0 ⇒ 50 / 25 / 0

Wukong

Wukong is not doing great in Season 2026, so Riot is trying to enable clever plays with his clones.

  • W – Warrior Trickster
    • Clone Damage: 30 / 35 / 40 / 45 / 50% ⇒ 40 / 45 / 50 / 55 / 60%
    • Clone Duration: 3.25 ⇒ 4
  • E – Nimbus Strike
    • Bonus Attack Speed: 35 / 40 / 45 / 50 / 55% ⇒ 40 / 45 / 50 / 55 / 60%

Zeri

Riot isn’t happy with Zeri’s current win-rate and is trying to make her a bit easier to play as players adapt to her current playstyle.

  • Q – Burst Fire
    • Base Damage: 21 / 24 / 27 / 30 / 33 ⇒ 22 / 26 / 30 / 34 / 38
    • Excess attack speed conversion to AD: 50% ⇒ 60%
  • R – Lightning Crash
    • (Bugfix) Overcharged duration: Attacks during R currently extend the R duration by 1.5s. Changing that to 2.5s to match the new Hypercharged MS stack duration.

LoL Patch 26.10 Champion Nerfs

Anivia

Anivia has been very popular in pro play, played in top, mid, and support.

Riot says her playstyle and build are mostly the same in each role, so is targeting her base stats, hoping that nerfs to Anivia’s survivability will particularly hurt her in the top lane.

  • Base Stats
    • Base Armor: 21 ⇒ 19
    • Armor Growth: 4.5 ⇒ 4.1

Ashe

Ashe’s damage is higher than Riot likes, saying she can’t have both loads of damage and utility.

  • Q – Ranger’s Focus
    • Total Damage: 110 / 117.5 / 125 / 132.5 / 140% AD ⇒ 110 / 115 / 120 / 125 / 130% AD

Naafiri

Naafiri is also being hit with nerfs are performing well in both mid and jungle.

  • Q – Darkin Daggers
    • Minimum Bonus Physical Damage: 30 / 45 / 60 / 75 / 90 (+ 40% bonus AD) -⇒ 30 / 42.5 / 55 / 67.5 / 80 (+ 40% bonus AD)
    • Maximum Bonus Physical Damage: 60 / 90 / 120 / 450 / 180 (+ 140% bonus AD) ⇒ 60 / 85 / 110 / 135 / 160 (+ 140% bonus AD)
  • R – Hounds’ Pursuit
    • Physical Damage: 150 / 250 / 350 (120% bonus AD ) + 15 / 25 / 35 (+12% bonus AD) per packmate ⇒ 150 / 225 / 300 (120% bonus AD) + 15 / 22.5 / 30 ( +12% bonus AD) per packmate

Shyvana

Shyvana is in the patch notes yet again, with her last changes finding her success when building more health and ability haste.

Riot is now looking to bring her innate tankiness and dragon power down a smidge.

  • Base Stats
    • Health per level: 100 ⇒ 95
  • W – Inferno Aegis
    • Cooldown: 13 / 12 / 11 / 10 / 9 ⇒ 13 / 12.25 / 11.5 / 10.75 / 10
    • Shield: 75 / 95 / 115 / 135 / 155 (+12% bonus health) ⇒ 60 / 80 / 100 / 120 / 140 (+12% bonus health)

Zed

Season 2 of 2026 has heavily benefited Zed, and Riot expects him to remain strong despite these nerfs.

  • P – Contempt for the Weak
    • Max Health Damage: 6 / 8 / 10% ⇒ 5 / 7.5 / 10%
  • E – Shadow Slash
    • Damage: 70 / 95 / 120 / 145 / 170 (+80% bonus AD) ⇒ 70 / 92.5 / 115 / 137.5 / 160 (+70% bonus AD)

26.10 Item Changes

Doran’s Bow

Introduced last patch, Riot is trying to give players more reasons to pick the new starting item that doesn’t grant health.

  • Attack Damage: 6 ⇒ 8

Doran’s Helm

Another new starting item that players don’t trust. It’s been too difficult to get out of the early game with this scaling item.

  • Health: 110 ⇒ 140

Gluttonous Greaves / Immortal Path

The new omnivamp boots have filled the hole Riot hoped, but a little too well.

  • Total Cost: 950g ⇒ 1000g
  • Slay – Omnivamp Per Stack: 1% per takedown, up to 6 stacks max ⇒ .6% per takedown, up to 10 stacks max

Lich Bane

Riot is buffing Lich Bane’s movement speed to help players get into position to fully make use of the item.

  • AP ratio: 40% AP ⇒ 45% AP
  • Movespeed: 4% ⇒ 6%

Voltaic Cyclosword

Riot is increasing Cyclosword’s price tag after it became a bit too powerful from the recent changes.

  • Total Cost: 2900g ⇒ 3000g

26.10 Runes Changes

Deathfire Touch

Even after the hotfix nerf, Deathfire Touch is still a little too strong in early laning trades.

  • Damage Per Second: 4 – 12 (Levels 1 – 18) (+7% bonus AD) (+2.5% AP) ⇒ 3 – 12 (Levels 1 – 18) (+7% bonus AD) (+2.5% AP)

Stormraider’s Surge

Stormraider’s Surge is underperforming on most champions trying it out, so Riot is going to push its duration a bit.

  • Move Speed: [40% Melee | 30% Ranged] for 3 seconds ⇒ [48% Melee | 36% Ranged] for 4 seconds

What do you make of the recent patch and the current meta? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

One big change coming outside of this patch is that Riot is experimenting to let LoL players queue in three roles with the League Next update.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android
